attrs.alt: Sign-In Log types and data flow: four log streams exported through diagnostic settings to Log Analytics, Storage, or Event Hub

content.text: Entra ID generates four separate sign-in log streams, each covering a different class of authentication actor. All four are available in the Entra ID portal under Monitoring and can be exported via diagnostic settings. Without export, each stream is retained for 30 days (Entra ID P1/P2) or 7 days (free tier).
User-initiated browser logins, app sign-ins with user prompt
Token refreshes, silent sign-ins, MSAL background auth flows
Client-credentials OAuth flows, app-only tokens (no user)
Azure VM, App Service, Functions authenticating via managed identity

Non-interactive sign-ins are often 10-100x higher volume than interactive sign-ins — token refreshes happen every hour for active sessions. Monitor non-interactive logs separately from interactive logs to avoid noise in security dashboards.
Each sign-in event is a rich JSON object with over 40 fields. These are the most operationally important for security monitoring and CA troubleshooting.
Name of the application being accessed
City, state, country resolved from IP
0 = success; nonzero codes map to AADSTS errors
Per-step auth method used and whether it succeeded
none / confirmedSafe / remediated / dismissedAsFixed / atRisk / confirmedCompromised
Device OS, browser, compliance state, join type

The auditLogs/signIns endpoint exposes interactive sign-in records. Use OData filters to scope to failures, specific users, apps, or time windows. Service principal and managed identity sign-ins are at separate endpoints.

# Read interactive sign-in logs via Graph API (last 24 hours, failures only)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'

$filter = "createdDateTime ge $(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ' (Get-Date).AddDays(-1)) and status/errorCode ne 0"
$signIns = Get-MgAuditLogSignIn -Filter $filter -Top 100
foreach ($s in $signIns) {
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Time     = $s.CreatedDateTime
        User     = $s.UserPrincipalName
        App      = $s.AppDisplayName
        IP       = $s.IpAddress
        Error    = $s.Status.ErrorCode
        Detail   = $s.Status.FailureReason
        CAStatus = $s.ConditionalAccessStatus
    }
} | Format-Table -AutoSize

# Read service principal sign-ins (app-to-app, no user)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/servicePrincipalSignIns?`$top=50" \
    -OutputType PSObject | Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object createdDateTime, servicePrincipalName, resourceDisplayName, ipAddress, status
Sign-in failures are identified by a numeric error code in status.errorCode (Graph) or ResultType (Log Analytics). These codes map to specific AADSTS error conditions with distinct root causes.
MFA required but not completed; check CA policy and MFA registration
CA policy requires MFA; user must complete it
User must register MFA before accessing this resource
Bad credentials; check for typos or leaked/changed password
Session persistence interrupted; user must re-authenticate
CA grant control returned block; no way to satisfy it
MFA registration blocked due to user risk — admin must remediate first
Token request scope not supported by the app; check app permissions
App not authorized for this flow; check app registration
Configuring Diagnostic Settings for Log Retention

The Entra ID portal's 30-day retention limit is insufficient for most compliance frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS). Enable diagnostic settings to stream all four log types to a Log Analytics workspace for long-term retention and cross-log correlation.

# Configure diagnostic settings to export sign-in logs to Log Analytics
# Required for retention > 30 days and Sentinel integration

$workspaceId = '/subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/<ws>'

$body = @{
    properties = @{
        workspaceId = $workspaceId
        logs = @(
            @{ category = 'SignInLogs'; enabled = $true },
            @{ category = 'NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs'; enabled = $true },
            @{ category = 'ServicePrincipalSignInLogs'; enabled = $true },
            @{ category = 'ManagedIdentitySignInLogs'; enabled = $true }
        )
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-AzRestMethod -Method PUT \
    -Path '/providers/Microsoft.Aadiam/diagnosticSettings/SignInLogs?api-version=2017-04-01-preview' \
    -Payload $body

# Verify the setting was created
Invoke-AzRestMethod -Method GET \
    -Path '/providers/Microsoft.Aadiam/diagnosticSettings?api-version=2017-04-01-preview' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty Content | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object name, properties
Log Analytics with the SigninLogs and related tables enables operational dashboards, CA policy troubleshooting, legacy auth identification, and compliance reporting.

// KQL: sign-in failures by error code (last 7 days)
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize Count = count() by ResultType, ResultDescription
| order by Count desc

// KQL: top apps by sign-in volume
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| summarize SignIns = count(), Users = dcount(UserPrincipalName) by AppDisplayName
| order by SignIns desc
| take 20

// KQL: sign-ins by legacy auth client type (block candidates)
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where ClientAppUsed in ('Exchange ActiveSync', 'IMAP4', 'POP3', 'SMTP', 'Other clients')
| summarize Count = count(), Users = dcount(UserPrincipalName) by ClientAppUsed, AppDisplayName
| order by Count desc

// KQL: CA policy failures — which policies are blocking users most often
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| mv-expand ConditionalAccessPolicies
| where ConditionalAccessPolicies.result == 'failure'
| summarize Failures = count() by PolicyName=tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.displayName)
| order by Failures desc

// KQL: non-interactive sign-in volume by app (token refreshes)
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count() by AppDisplayName
| order by Count desc

A common high-value Sentinel analytics rule uses sign-in log geo-coordinates to detect impossible travel — two successful sign-ins from the same user, from locations more than N km apart, within a short time window.

// KQL: Sentinel analytics rule — impossible travel detection alert
// Alert when the same user signs in from two locations > 500km apart within 1 hour
let distance_threshold_km = 500;
let time_window = 1h;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where ResultType == 0
| where isnotempty(Location)
| project UserPrincipalName, TimeGenerated, City=Location,
          Lat=toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.latitude),
          Lon=toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.longitude), IPAddress
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where isnotempty(Location)
    | project UserPrincipalName, TimeGenerated2=TimeGenerated, City2=Location,
              Lat2=toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.latitude),
              Lon2=toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.longitude), IPAddress2=IPAddress
  ) on UserPrincipalName
| where abs(TimeGenerated - TimeGenerated2) <= time_window
| where TimeGenerated != TimeGenerated2
| where IPAddress != IPAddress2
| extend DistKm = geo_distance_2points(Lon, Lat, Lon2, Lat2) / 1000
| where DistKm > distance_threshold_km
| project UserPrincipalName, TimeGenerated, City, IPAddress, TimeGenerated2, City2, IPAddress2, DistKm
| order by DistKm desc
CA Policy Troubleshooting with Sign-In Logs

The conditionalAccessPolicies array in each sign-in record contains the per-policy evaluation result, including which grant controls were enforced and whether the policy succeeded or failed. This makes sign-in logs the primary tool for diagnosing CA policy misconfigurations.

// KQL: full CA evaluation breakdown for a specific user's recent sign-ins
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where UserPrincipalName == 'alice@contoso.com'
| mv-expand ConditionalAccessPolicies
| project TimeGenerated,
          AppDisplayName,
          PolicyName = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.displayName),
          PolicyResult = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.result),
          GrantControls = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.enforcedGrantControls),
          SessionControls = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.enforcedSessionControls),
          ResultType
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// KQL: identify which CA policy blocked a specific sign-in (by correlation ID)
SigninLogs
| where CorrelationId == '<correlation-id-from-error-page>'
| mv-expand ConditionalAccessPolicies
| where ConditionalAccessPolicies.result in ('failure', 'reportOnlyFailure')
| project PolicyName = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.displayName),
          PolicyResult = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.result),
          GrantControls = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies.enforcedGrantControls)

The authenticationDetails array records each authentication step: which method was used, whether it succeeded, and the result detail. Use these fields to measure MFA adoption, identify users still on single-factor, and understand which MFA methods are driving your authentication strength requirements.

// KQL: breakdown of authentication methods used per step in multi-factor sign-ins
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 0
| mv-expand AuthenticationDetails
| extend Step = tostring(AuthenticationDetails.authenticationStepResultDetail)
| extend Method = tostring(AuthenticationDetails.authenticationMethod)
| extend Succeeded = tostring(AuthenticationDetails.succeeded)
| summarize Count = count() by Method, Succeeded
| order by Count desc

// KQL: find users still using password as sole authentication factor (no MFA)
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 0
| where AuthenticationRequirement == 'singleFactorAuthentication'
| summarize Count = count() by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName
| order by Count desc

// KQL: MFA method distribution across successful sign-ins
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where ResultType == 0
| where AuthenticationRequirement == 'multiFactorAuthentication'
| mv-expand AuthenticationDetails
| where tostring(AuthenticationDetails.succeeded) == 'true'
| where tostring(AuthenticationDetails.authenticationMethod) != 'Previously satisfied'
| summarize Count = count() by Method=tostring(AuthenticationDetails.authenticationMethod)
| order by Count desc
Service Principal and Managed Identity Log Analysis

Service principal sign-in volume is a leading indicator of app health and a potential signal for compromised client credentials. Sudden volume spikes or sign-ins from unexpected IP addresses warrant investigation.

// KQL: service principal sign-ins by app — detect unusual volume spikes
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count() by ServicePrincipalName, ResourceDisplayName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Count > 100  // tune threshold per app
| order by TimeGenerated desc, Count desc

// KQL: service principal sign-ins from unexpected IPs
// (useful for detecting stolen client credentials used from attacker infrastructure)
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| summarize IPAddresses = make_set(IPAddress) by ServicePrincipalName
| where array_length(IPAddresses) > 3  // apps normally sign in from 1-2 IPs
| order by array_length(IPAddresses) desc

// KQL: managed identity sign-ins that failed (usually config errors)
AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d)
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize Count = count() by ServicePrincipalName, ResourceDisplayName, ResultType, ResultDescription
| order by Count desc

Choose the export destination based on how you plan to use the logs. Log Analytics is the recommended choice for organizations using Microsoft Sentinel; Storage Account is cheapest for long-term compliance archiving; Event Hub is required for real-time streaming to external SIEM tools.
Default 30 days; configurable up to 12 years
Compliance archiving; JSON files in blob storage
Configurable retention (up to 7 days in hub)
Real-time stream to external SIEM (Splunk, QRadar, ArcSight)

For forensic investigations, you may need to export sign-in logs to CSV before they age out of the 30-day window. The Graph API paginates results using @odata.nextLink — follow the link until it is null to retrieve the complete set.

# Bulk export sign-in logs via Graph API to CSV
# Useful for point-in-time forensic exports before the 30-day window closes

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'

$startDate = (Get-Date).AddDays(-7).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')
$endDate = (Get-Date).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')
$filter = "createdDateTime ge $startDate and createdDateTime le $endDate"

$allSignIns = @()
$uri = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/signIns?`$filter=$filter&`$top=1000"

do {
    $response = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri $uri -OutputType PSObject
    $allSignIns += $response.value
    $uri = $response.'@odata.nextLink'
    Write-Host "Fetched $($allSignIns.Count) records so far..."
} while ($uri)

$allSignIns | Select-Object createdDateTime, userPrincipalName, appDisplayName,
    ipAddress, @{N='errorCode';E={$_.status.errorCode}},
    @{N='failureReason';E={$_.status.failureReason}},
    conditionalAccessStatus, riskLevelDuringSignIn |
    Export-Csv -Path './signins_export.csv' -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "Exported $($allSignIns.Count) records to signins_export.csv"
Four sign-in log types cover all authentication actors: interactive users, non-interactive (token refresh), service principals, and managed identities.
Default retention is 30 days (P1/P2); configure diagnostic settings to export to Log Analytics for long-term retention and Sentinel integration.
The status.errorCode field maps directly to AADSTS error codes — the first diagnostic step for any sign-in failure investigation.
Non-interactive sign-ins are high volume (token refreshes) — monitor separately from interactive logs to avoid alert fatigue.
KQL queries against SigninLogs enable CA troubleshooting, legacy auth detection, and compliance reporting in one unified query surface.
The Sentinel impossible travel analytics rule is a foundational detection built directly from sign-in log geo-coordinates.
